title: Table 3 in Detection and characterization of diverse coccidian protozoa shed by California sea lions
author: Girard, Yvette A.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Table 3  Prevalence and genetic diversity of coccidian species identified by microscopy in fecal samples of free-ranging California sea lions at coastal haul-out sites.     Location (Sample identifier)  No. samples examined  Time period  No. oocyst-positive samples  Sample ID (Coccidian species)        for oocysts    (Prevalence)      White Rock (WR-)  94  July 2011  ‾  August 2012  3 (3.2%)  WR101 (Coccidia A)            WR111 (uncharacterized) a            WR121 (Coccidia A)    Ano ̃Nuevo (̃Ano-)  48  May 2011  ‾  October 2011  4 (8.3%)  ̃Ano11 (novel)b            Ãno44 (Coccidia B)            Ãno50 (Coccidia B)            Ãno53 (Coccidia A)    Point Lobos (PL-)  70  August 2011  ‾  Sept 2012  6 (8.6%)  PL11 (Coccidia B +  S. neurona  -like c)            PL 27 (Coccidia A +  S. neurona  -like c)            PL50 (Coccidia A)            PL58 (Coccidia B)            PL68 (Coccidia A)            PL75 (Coccidia B)    Total  244    13 (5.3%)  6 Coccidia A            5 Coccidia B            2  S. neurona  -like            1 uncharacterized a            1 novel b      a  Oocysts were identified by microscopy during isolation, but DNA was insufficient for molecular characterization.   b  The amplified ITS-1 region of ̃Ano11 is genetically distinct from other coccidian isolates (See Fig. 1).   c  The presence of  Sarcocystis neurona  -like DNA was confirmed by PCR and sequence analysis. Sporocysts were not identified in fecal samples by microscopy.

Sporocysts were not identified in fecal samples by microscopy.
